concerns.text: Tasks T029, T030, T032, T039, T040, and T041 introduce Expected Calibration Error (ECE) and confidence extraction logic. The spec (FR-001 to FR-013) and Success Criteria (SC-001 to SC-007) define functional correctness (pass@1) as the sole dependent variable. There is no requirement, acceptance criterion, or success metric for measuring model confidence or calibration. This constitutes unapproved scope creep and a semantic weakening of the study's defined scope by introducing a new primary metric not mandated by the spec.

Task T037 implements sensitivity analysis across 'a range of high-confidence values.' Spec FR-009 and FR-013 explicitly mandate a specific threshold set of {0.85, 0.90, 0.95, 0.99}. The task's vague phrasing allows for a different sweep (e.g., {0.80, 0.85, 0.90}) which would fail to satisfy the specific survivorship bias measurement required by the spec, effectively relaxing the acceptance criteria.

Task T019 references 'budget cap logic... per FR-011'. FR-011 states the system must limit generations to 'a sufficient number of samples' but does not define a numeric cap or a specific deterministic ordering rule beyond 'prioritizing original prompts'. The task assumes a specific implementation detail (deterministic ordering by task_id then perturbation_type) that is not explicitly mandated by the spec, risking a silent drift in the definition of 'sufficient' and 'deterministic'.

Task T024 specifies 'StarCoder2-3B loading... with CPU offload'. The spec (FR-004) mandates 'CPU with offload' but the plan (Complexity Tracking) notes 'Reduced Sample Size (1 perturbation/task)' as a constraint to meet the 6-hour limit. The tasks do not explicitly encode the '1 perturbation/task' constraint derived from the plan's feasibility justification, risking the generation of 3 perturbations per task (as per FR-002) which would violate the implicit feasibility constraint established in the plan.
